title: Living in Boulder Vlog 4 - 4 - 2017: 10,000 kilometer diameter backgrounds

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs at nearly 2am on April 5th, 2017, recapping his day: he got up on little sleep and spent the day troubleshooting Adobe Dreamweaver batch-edit issues while building a website with dimension/circle data visualizations. He also reflects on listening to Livy (the Roman historian), finding inspiration for a show he wants to make.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Thinks Livy is a really good writer who describes events well without over-explaining
Found the support helpful but entry-level; the fix (restarting the app) was simple
References Livy's argument that Alexander would have beaten Rome, but notes he was ultimately just a drunk — seems to find the debate interesting rather than taking a firm side
Decided a complete grid is better for learning despite extra work

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder, V-Log, for the 4th of April, 2017.
Right now it's 2 in the morning, 1.56 in the morning of April 5th.
I'm distracting myself, let me minimize this.
Sometimes I get distracted by that, okay.
Okay, so, yeah, okay, so today I got up at like 7.30.
I just got up and started working at 7.30, even though I was up until like 3.
So, you know, like, that's how it is with me.
I'll get like 8 hours of sleep for like a week, and then all of a sudden one day I'll get 4, you know, for no reason.
Like, I'm not tired, I feel the fine, the same.
So it's cool, got some extra hours, but I needed it.
Yeah, I was still working on the same page.
Switched it to 20 area dimensions, and I was working on links for the charts.
I don't have those charts in their own pages, but I screwed up the colors because I couldn't do a batch edit
Because I took some columns out, and I wanted to batch edit, you know, the percentages of the columns, and I wouldn't do it.
And so I called Adobe Tech Support, and they got me on, you know, the Indian.
I've done this a few times, and it's just some Indians who don't really know it very well.
They'd like to hire them.
It's like entry level just to like kind of help you figure it out, but it was fun because they usually help me figure it out for myself.
But, yeah, we realized all I had to do is restart Dreamweaver, and then it worked.
I never had that problem with the old version of Dreamweaver, but so I did a batch edit of that,
And then I did a batch edit of the colors because I had to switch colors.
But I forgot that I had the other one for the race distances below it, and so it screwed up the colors on that,
And I couldn't do a batch edit because it wouldn't do batch edits anymore even when I would restart it,
And it wouldn't have really worked that well anyway because the way it screwed up the color side manually,
So that took a lot of time.
And then I had to finish building it.
Like right now I'm building 250 meters diameter of like Guyana because I, you know, it was partially completed.
I didn't know that I wanted to do this completely.
I was like, yeah, we'll do samples.
It's like now you don't do samples because when you make the, you know, the grid,
It has to be like a square grid, like a complete grid.
It can't be incomplete, but, you know.
So, yeah, but it's also better for learning to be able to zoom in and out completely of all of them.
So maybe I'll be done with it tomorrow, hopefully.
I was listening to, I'm listening to Livy again.
Livy, when I pay attention to it, is fascinating.
I think Livy, number one, is a really good writer, and he does a really good job of just describing the events really well and the situation
And allowing you to imagine, you know, fill in the blanks and imagine what's going on by commenting on stuff,
But not, you know, over explaining and just telling a story.
Yeah, and, I mean, it's a 3,300-page book, so every time you read it, you learn about more things than me because I'm, like, high all the time.
I'm daydreaming when I listen to it.
But, yeah, so I'm going to start listening to it a lot because I get ideas.
I want to get ideas for my show, you know, and the more I think about it now, the better I'll do when I make the show.
For example, they had to, the first guy, the second king, made their calendar, and every 20 years, they would adjust the year,
So I would do something like that every 20 years, and the Bolderon time would be six months.
So, here's the religious hat for Bolderon.
Yeah, I'll wear all of my hats, each hat, each day a different hat from now on.
Oh, backgrounds is 10,000 kilometers.
That's the last of the circles page.
Oh, my hat, scratch itches, though, when I put the hat on.
So, I don't know what I'll do after this.
I think I'm going to do a whole bunch of Google Earth, all my Google Earth collection,
And then when I'm done, I'll do my web page, you know.
But I'll try not to scroll up and down too much so it doesn't give people headaches.
I lost, like, 25 subscribers because of these, I think.
Anyway, yeah, so, yeah, looking forward to the Libby.
He was talking about whether or not Alexander the Great would have been able to kick Roman's ass,
And he's like, no, I didn't.
He gave, like, all these reasons why Alexander the Great would have gotten his ass in the tomb if he'd ever tried to fuck with Rome.
So, yeah, I'll incorporate that into my show.
They said because he wasn't really a good general, because he was just a drunk.
And they're in, like, each and every Roman soldier was just because, you know.
Anyway, yeah, there are a lot of cool things.
Anyway, so I guess I'll get to sleep, or maybe I'll work a little bit more.
Oh, but I was doing a little bit of, I listened to an interview with Jane Mayer.
Learned a little bit more about that.
That's one of the projects I'm working on.
Anyways, I think I've talked enough.
I have nothing else to talk about.
Oh, 614 is kind of a, all right, peace out.
